paragraphs.body: On this day it was 90 degrees, humid, and I walking down Cardiac hill headed to the School of Public Health. I was almost overwhelmed by the loud bangs from the construction, horns beeping, and seeing students speed walking to not be late. I could feel my calves begin to tighten and my skin became sticky because of the humidity the longer. I walked I entered through the front doors and immediately was hit with the dead silence of the building, not even a whisper. As I turned the left corner there was a pair of glass doors that I could see straight through and as I pulled them open, I could hear the rubber on the bottom break the silence just a little bit by scrapping against the tile. I then sunk into the spongy chair waiting for my advisor meeting. I then heard a soft voice reminding me that I need to sign in, so I walked over to check in. As I watched my key card swipe the lady at the front desk offered me candy, so I walked over to her desk and saw a little candy bowl. As I could hear the rustling of the candy wrappers, I finally saw one that appealed to me the most. The one I chose had a light pink and red wrapper. Before I saw what the candy was called, I heard a voice say, “Paisley,” so I threw the candy into the left pocket of my bookbag and followed my advisor back to her office. After my meeting, I began to walk back up cardiac hill, which made my whole legs begin to start aching, so to distract myself I decided to pull out the piece of candy. After digging in my pocket, feeling past the old, sticky gum wrappers, and the cold loose change, I found the candy that read, “Strawberry Crème Saver.” I took it out of the wrapper and placed it on my tongue. I immediately tasted the sweet strawberry flavor that tings on my tongue, mixed with what feels like a soft crème that mixes perfectly with the strawberry. As soon as I got off the second set of the escalators in the Peterson Event Center, the saver was gone. I immediately needed another one. It’s like it released dopamine in my brain. It was as if in that moment of time my worries were gone, and I was just on my way back to my dorm enjoying the present.

annotations.text: immediately needed another one. It’s like it released dopamine in my brain. It was as if in that moment of time my worries were gone, and I was just on my way back to my dorm enjoying the present.
annotations.comment: Interesting. I wonder what the metaphor of the candy is? Let's see :)...
Great sensory descriptions throughout this narrative paragraph!

paragraphs.body: When I first applied to the University of Pittsburgh, I was so nervous I wasn’t going to be accepted. I constantly anxious about my grades and worried I wasn’t going to be good enough. It was during the month of September, on a colder day. I was driving with four of my best friends to the Pittsburgh Mills. We decided that we wanted to get Starbucks, so we pulled into the drive-thru line. As we waited to order we all began to check our bank accounts and dug through our wallets for money. We were laughing and making jokes about how we were all so broke and spending our last $12 on drinks and cake pops. After we ordered, we turned the corner to then pull up to the pay window, but the car in front of us was taking so long. We began to complain and become impatient not realizing what that car was going to do. The car in front of us had decided to pay for all our drinks and treats without us even knowing them. Our total was more than likely around $50. We were so happy and grateful that someone was so kind. We felt horrible for complaining and then made jokes that maybe they had heard us talking about how broke we were. As the car was pulling away, I saw a Pitt sticker in their back window. That sticker pulled a weight off my chest. It gave me a sense of hope, and little did I know a few weeks later I would be accepted into the University of Pittsburgh.

annotations.text: We were so happy and grateful that someone was so kind. We felt horrible for complaining and then made jokes that maybe they had heard us talking about how broke we were. As the car was pulling away, I saw a Pitt sticker in their back window. That sticker pulled a weight off my chest. It gave me a sense of hope, and little did I know a few weeks later I would be accepted into the University of Pittsburgh.
annotations.comment: Oh yes. I see a pattern emerging. Something about how the little things make students feel included and validated and wanted. Let's talk about it!

paragraphs.body: The word “purpose” stands as a noun. According to the Etymology Dictionary, the definition is “intention, aim, goal; object to be kept in view; proper function for which something exists.” The word “purpose” relates to everyone, no matter their age, race, income status, we all have a purpose to do something in this world and that’s one of the things that connects everyone. A purpose may be something you say, give, how you treat somebody, etc. A purpose can be the act of just about almost anything, however, there is always an intention behind it. I believe that everyone has more than one purpose in life, no matter how little or large those purposes maybe they all have an impact. A purpose should not be intended to harm someone, however, maybe if some you love causes harm in your life there is a deeper purpose for all the pain you may experience. A small purpose may be smiling at a stranger, you may not know it but maybe that stranger was having a tough day and you just cheered them up by a simple, but kind gesture. I think that people overlook how important to be to just be kind. I tend to get so caught up worrying about the future and creating scenarios in my head just to make me stressed out about problems that may arise, that I forget the purpose behind the little things. I believe everyone, in some stage of their life, overlook the little joys that are right in front of us.

annotations.text: think that people overlook how important to be to just be kind.
annotations.context_right: I tend to get so ca

annotations.text: small purpose may be smiling at a stranger, you may not know it but maybe that stranger was having a tough day and you just cheered them up by a simple, but kind gesture.

paragraphs.body: Today, when I enter in the School of Public Health, I take a left I wait to see the kind lady working the front desk, but if she is not there, I ring the bell. She turns the corner with a big smile on her face and asks, “You’re here for the strawberry crème candies, aren’t you?” And I respond by saying “Yes, I am.” She and the other people that work in there now know who I am because I always stop in and say hello and grab a strawberry crème saver. But on this specific day I came in, she told me she had something for me. She pulled out a roll of strawberry crème savers and told me that the lady that buys her them bought me some too. This absolutely made my day. These little moments in my life have a purpose I cherish being able to go to such an amazing college and meeting such kind people. I know that when I’m having a bad day, I can go into the student affairs office and be treated with nothing but kindness. The sad thing is still since I started college in August, I still do not know her name, but I could never not recognize her face. She has made some of my bad days turn better and brought a smile to my face just by serving a little purpose in my life. I think everyone needs to talk a step back and appreciate the moments in life that are full of purpose. If you start to pay attention, you’ll notice them too.

annotations.text: This absolutely made my day. These little moments in my life have a purpose I cherish being able to go to such an amazing college and meeting such kind people.
